items.title: As workers worry about AI, Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says AI is ‘creating an enormous number of jobs’
items.summary: The Nvidia CEO seems to feel that claims of AI's job-killing potential have been greatly exaggerated.

items.title: Geothermal startup Fervo Energy to raise up to $1.3B in IPO
items.summary: Enhanced geothermal startup Fervo Energy’s IPO could value the company at up to $6.5 billion.

items.title: US government warns of severe CopyFail bug affecting major versions of Linux
items.summary: U.S. cybersecurity agency CISA says the CopyFail bug is being actively used in hacking campaigns, and poses a major risk to servers and data centers that rely on Linux.

items.title: OpenAI’s cozy partner Cerebras is on track for a blockbuster IPO
items.summary: AI chip maker Cerebras is heading for a blockbuster IPO that could value it at $26.6 billion or more. Its relationship with OpenAI is deep and rich.

items.title: Image AI models now drive app growth, beating chatbot upgrades
items.summary: Appfigures finds visual model launches generate 6.5x more downloads — but most don’t convert that spike into revenue.

items.title: Katie Haun raises $1B for new venture funds
items.summary: Katie Haun announced on Monday that $1 billion has been raised across new funds to continue the firm’s thesis of backing crypto and blockchain startups.

items.title: Hackers are mass-exploiting the cPanel bug to gain control of thousands of websites
items.summary: Days after the disclosure of a critical vulnerability in popular web hosting software cPanel and WHM, hackers are now targeting and hacking thousands of vulnerable websites.

items.title: Elon Musk’s only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race
items.summary: Stuart Russell is a long-time AI researcher who thinks governments need to restrain frontier labs.

items.title: Sierra raises $950M as the race to own enterprise AI gets serious
items.summary: The raise gives Sierra more than $1 billion to work with — capital the company says it will use to become the "global standard" for AI-powered customer experiences.

items.title: Elon Musk sent ominous texts to Greg Brockman, Sam Altman after asking for a settlement, OpenAI claims
items.summary: Musk texted OpenAI's president and co-founder saying that he and CEO Sam Altman "will be the most hated men in America" if OpenAI doesn't settle the suit.

items.title: Anthropic and OpenAI are both launching joint ventures for enterprise AI services
items.summary: Both Anthropic and OpenAI have partnered with asset managers to more aggressively market their enterprise AI products.

items.title: As X shuts down Communities, Acorn debuts an alternative that puts creators in control
items.summary: Acorn lets organizations build and run their own online communities using decentralized tech, with custom feeds, moderation, and analytics tools.

items.title: US healthcare marketplaces shared citizenship and race data with ad tech giants
items.summary: Virginia and Washington, D.C. paused the data collection and sharing, after Bloomberg's investigation found their health insurance marketplaces were sharing users' information with advertisers.

items.title: Amazon opens up its global logistics network to all businesses
items.summary: The new service, called Amazon Supply Chain Services, pits the e-commerce giant directly against UPS and FedEx.

items.title: 5 days only: Bring a partner or colleague and get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
items.summary: The BOGO offer is live. For a limited time, buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. Offer ends this Friday, May 8. Save here.

items.title: Barocal can cool your food and drink by squeezing a hunk of plastic crystals
items.summary: Barocal might have discovered a cheap, nonpolluting material that could dethrone today's refrigeration technology.

items.title: DoorDash adds AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding, edit photos of dishes
items.summary: DoorDash on Monday added new AI-powered tools that let merchants speed up onboarding, edit photos to make dishes look better, and create new websites from existing content.

items.title: Ouster’s new color lidar is coming to replace cameras
items.summary: A sensor that can simultaneously capture depth and image data has long been a "holy grail," Ouster CEO Angus Pacala told TechCrunch.

items.title: Nicolas Sauvage is betting on the boring parts of AI
items.summary: The portfolio he has assembled since 2019 is dotted with technologies that have become more widely interesting to VCs over the last year.

items.title: We’ll take it: A TikToker rallies pledges to buy Spirit Airlines after its abrupt weekend collapse
items.summary: Within hours he'd thrown up a website — a janky, one-hour job, by his own admission — and by Sunday, 36,000 "founding patrons" had pledged nearly $23 million, crashing his servers in the process.
